gruke
Table of Contents

Class DockerTasks

Namespace
Nuke.Common.Tools.Docker
Assembly
Nuke.Common.dll

Docker is an open platform for developing, shipping, and running applications. Docker enables you to separate your applications from your infrastructure so you can deliver software quickly. With Docker, you can manage your infrastructure in the same ways you manage your applications. By taking advantage of Docker’s methodologies for shipping, testing, and deploying code quickly, you can significantly reduce the delay between writing code and running it in production.

For more details, visit the official website.

[PublicAPI]
[ExcludeFromCodeCoverage]
[LogLevelPattern(LogEventLevel.Warning, "^WARNING!")]
public class DockerTasks : ToolTasks, IRequirePathTool, IRequireTool

Docker(ArgumentStringHandler, string, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string>, int?, bool?, bool?, Action<OutputType, string>, Func<IProcess, object>)

Docker is an open platform for developing, shipping, and running applications. Docker enables you to separate your applications from your infrastructure so you can deliver software quickly. With Docker, you can manage your infrastructure in the same ways you manage your applications. By taking advantage of Docker’s methodologies for shipping, testing, and deploying code quickly, you can significantly reduce the delay between writing code and running it in production.

For more details, visit the official website.

public static IReadOnlyCollection<Output> Docker(ArgumentStringHandler arguments, string workingDirectory = null, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string> environmentVariables = null, int? timeout = null, bool? logOutput = null, bool? logInvocation = null, Action<OutputType, string> logger = null, Func<IProcess, object> exitHandler = null)

Parameters

arguments ArgumentStringHandler
workingDirectory string
environmentVariables IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string>
timeout int?
logOutput bool?
logInvocation bool?
logger Action<OutputType, string>
exitHandler Func<IProcess, object>

Returns

IReadOnlyCollection<Output>

DockerAttach(CombinatorialConfigure<DockerAttachSettings>, int, bool)

Attach local standard input, output, and error streams to a running container.

For more details, visit the official website.

public static IEnumerable<(DockerAttachSettings Settings, IReadOnlyCollection<Output> Output)> DockerAttach(CombinatorialConfigure<DockerAttachSettings> configurator, int degreeOfParallelism = 1, bool completeOnFailure = false)

Parameters

configurator CombinatorialConfigure<DockerAttachSettings>
degreeOfParallelism int
completeOnFailure bool

Returns

IEnumerable<(DockerAttachSettings Settings, IReadOnlyCollection<Output> Output)>

Remarks

This is a CLI wrapper with fluent API that allows to modify the following arguments:

  • <container> via Container
  • --config via Config
  • --debug via Debug
  • --detach-keys via DetachKeys
  • --log-level via LogLevel
  • --no-stdin via NoStdin
  • --sig-proxy via SigProxy
  • --tls via TLS
  • --tlscacert via TLSCaCert
  • --tlscert via TLSCert
  • --tlskey via TLSKey
  • --tlsverify via TLSVerify

Parameters

configurator CombinatorialConfigure<DockerBuildSettings>
degreeOfParallelism int
completeOnFailure bool

Returns

IEnumerable<(DockerBuildSettings Settings, IReadOnlyCollection<Output> Output)>

Remarks

This is a CLI wrapper with fluent API that allows to modify the following arguments:

  • <path> via Path
  • --add-host via AddHost
  • --build-arg via BuildArg
  • --cache-from via CacheFrom
  • --cgroup-parent via CgroupParent
  • --compress via Compress
  • --config via Config

  • --disable-content-trust via DisableContentTrust

  • --memory via Memory

completeOnFailure bool

  • <args> via Args

DockerServiceInspect(CombinatorialConfigure<DockerServiceInspectSettings>, int, bool)

Display detailed information on one or more services.

For more details, visit the official website.

public static IEnumerable<(DockerServiceInspectSettings Settings, IReadOnlyCollection<Output> Output)> DockerServiceInspect(CombinatorialConfigure<DockerServiceInspectSettings> configurator, int degreeOfParallelism = 1, bool completeOnFailure = false)

Parameters

configurator CombinatorialConfigure<DockerServiceInspectSettings>
degreeOfParallelism int
completeOnFailure bool

Returns

IEnumerable<(DockerServiceInspectSettings Settings, IReadOnlyCollection<Output> Output)>

Remarks

  • <services> via Services

  • --debug via Debug

  • --pretty via Pretty
  • --tls via TLS

  • --tlskey via TLSKey
  • --tlsverify via TLSVerify

This is a CLI wrapper with fluent API that allows to modify the following arguments:

  • <containers> via Containers
  • --all via All
  • --config via Config
  • --debug via Debug
  • --format via Format
  • --log-level via LogLevel
  • --no-stream via NoStream
  • --no-trunc via NoTrunc
  • --tls via TLS
  • --tlscacert via TLSCaCert
  • --tlscert via TLSCert
  • --tlskey via TLSKey
  • --tlsverify via TLSVerify

degreeOfParallelism int
completeOnFailure bool

  • --log-level via LogLevel
  • --max-snapshots via MaxSnapshots
  • --snapshot-interval via SnapshotInterval
  • --task-history-limit via TaskHistoryLimit

  • --tlskey via TLSKey
  • --tlsverify via TLSVerify

  • --yes via Yes
